categories.name: Mathematics (Required)
categories.description: Calculus I, Calculus II, and Introduction to Probability Theory are all required.

categories.name: Mathematics (Linear Algebra)
categories.description: Choose one: AM 10 or MATH 21 for the linear algebra requirement.

categories.name: Chemistry
categories.description: Select one of the following General Chemistry series.

categories.name: Laboratory Courses
categories.description: Complete both laboratory courses: Introduction to Basic Laboratory Techniques (BME 21L) and Foundations of Design and Experimentation in Molecular Biology (BME 22L).

categories.name: Physics (Introductory)
categories.description: Choose one: PHYS 5A/L (Introduction to Physics I) or PHYS 15A/5L (Honors Introduction to Physics I).

categories.name: Physics (Required)
categories.description: Introduction to Physics II is required.

categories.name: Biology (Required)
categories.description: Cell and Molecular Biology (BIOL 20A) is required.

categories.name: Genetics
categories.description: Choose one: BME 105 (Genetics in the Genomics Era, strongly recommended) or BIOL 105 (Genetics).

categories.name: Biochemistry
categories.description: Select one of the following Biochemistry series.

categories.name: Bioinformatics and Bioethics
categories.description: Complete all Bioinformatics and Bioethics courses. BME 80G (Bioethics), BME 160 (Research Programming in the Life Sciences, 6 units), BME 110 (Computational Biology Tools), BME 185 (Technical Writing for Biomolecular Engineers, also satisfies DC requirement), BME 163 (Applied Visualization and Analysis of Scientific Data).

categories.name: Modeling & Design Sequence

groups.label: Genomes / Long Read Sequencing: BME 130 + BME 123L

categories.description: Choose one of the following Modeling & Design sequences.

categories.name: Elective
categories.description: Choose one elective. Course used as an Elective cannot be used to satisfy other major requirements. Any 5-credit biomolecular engineering graduate course (BME 201-279) may also be used. *BIOL 115 and BME 178 have additional prerequisites not covered by the major requirements.

categories.name: Biomolecular Capstone
categories.description: Students must complete one of the following capstone tracks. The Bioinformatics Capstone is programming heavy. The Senior Thesis option consists of 15 credits of BME 195 split over 3 quarters.
